_meta.description: Telegram gateway test fixtures — message payloads for functional validation
_meta.note: Telegram tests require a TEST_CHAT_ID env var pointing to a non-production chat (e.g., a dedicated test bot conversation or a private test channel). Never use production chat IDs in validate scripts. The validate_telegram.py script must skip send tests if TEST_CHAT_ID is not set — logging a WARN, not a FAIL.

message_payloads.name: plain_text_message
message_payloads.description: Basic text message — confirms send endpoint is functional.

payload.message: Platform validation test — plain text. Timestamp: {timestamp}

message_payloads.name: structured_alert_message
message_payloads.description: Simulates a platform alert format — validates the pattern used by monitoring.

payload.message:
🔔 *Platform Alert Test*

Service: `validate-harness`
Status: OK
Timestamp: {timestamp}

This is a test message from the platform validate suite\.

TEST_CHAT_ID.description: Non-production Telegram chat ID for test messages
TEST_CHAT_ID.skip_behavior: If not set, send tests are SKIPPED (WARN) — not FAILED

TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN.description: Bot token for the Telegram gateway
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN.skip_behavior: If not set, all tests FAIL immediately
